body.title: Habit chunking — repeated action sequences compile into a single procedural habit
body.result: habit_chunker.py: scan 500 PROC_ trails, find 3-grams ≥5 occurrences, store as CHUNK_ PROCEDURAL memories. Live run found 8 chunks. PROC_CHUNK_INSPECTOR seeded. 17 tests. D277 gap closed.
body.description: D277 gap. Biological pattern: basal ganglia procedural learning compresses repeated sequences into single chunks. Igor implementation: ring buffer inspector in NE (runs weekly or on demand). Scans last 200 turns; finds sequences of 3+ habits that co-occur in same order 5+ times. Creates a new PROCEDURAL memory that chains them: action_sequence=[habit_A_id, habit_B_id, habit_C_id], trigger=AND(habit_A_trigger_keywords). Effectively compiles repeated patterns into a meta-habit. Complex: requires ring buffer sequence mining, new habit schema fields. Related: T-self-training-loop, T-trails-infra. Inertia: MEDIUM. Priority: low — implement after trails infra exists (trails give the sequence signal).
